summary: iter21 — Full end-to-end testing of the Slack/Discord webhook bridge (webhook_ext.py) + lockdown regression. Backend: 14/14 pytest cases PASS against the live preview env. Frontend: all 8 selectors (webhook-settings-card, webhook-url-input, webhook-save, webhook-current-host, webhook-enabled-toggle, webhook-send-test, webhook-clear, webhook-card-loading) render and behave correctly. Real httpbin.org/post send_event round-trip confirmed (returns {sent:true}, sonner success toast renders). Lockdown 503 path verified for /api/auth/register; login lockdown verified at code-path level (server.py:662 — lockdown is checked AFTER password verification by design, so a wrong-password attempt under lockdown returns 401, but a real preview user without correct creds is unavailable in test env). Final state of preview env: webhook cleared, lockdown=false, banner_enabled=false.
critical_code_review_comments: webhook_ext._get_webhook stores webhook_url + webhook_enabled on the SAME settings doc ('_id': 'payment_settings') as Stripe/PayPal/etc. Works fine, but worth documenting — a future operator-settings-clear endpoint that wipes the whole doc would also wipe the webhook. Consider isolating into its own doc (e.g. _id='alerts_settings') if this gets more fields.

send_event uses urllib via urlparse for host masking + httpx with timeout=8s for actual POST — solid. It's also strictly fire-and-forget (catches Exception and logs at WARNING) which is the right call for a non-critical alert path. Good.

Lockdown send_event calls (server.py:563-566 and 676-679) are correctly wrapped in their own try/except and re-imported inline — defensive: a webhook bug can never break the 503 path. Verified via the 503 register regression test.
PUT /api/operator/webhook re-uses the existing payment_settings doc with $set — non-webhook fields are preserved (verified manually: stripe_secret_key_set etc. unchanged after webhook PUTs).

Frontend WebhookSettingsCard.jsx is well-factored: optimistic toggle with rollback on error, write-only URL field (placeholder cleared after save), confirm() on destructive remove, send-test disabled when not configured or disabled. Good a11y via Switch component.

Minor: WebhookSettingsCard `if (loading) return <Loader>` returns BEFORE the section wrapper — the parent SettingsTab `<Section icon={Webhook} title="Slack / Discord alert webhook">` still renders the heading + chrome, so this is harmless. No fix needed.

Minor (nit): payload in send_event always sends BOTH 'text' (Slack) and 'content' (Discord) keys. Slack ignores 'content' and Discord ignores 'text' — fine, but a strict-mode generic JSON endpoint that rejects unknown keys would 4xx. httpbin.org/post accepts it. Document this dual-key shim in the helper docstring.
test_credentials: Operator: rac.investments.swe@gmail.com / REDACTED (no TOTP). Skip button on /setup-2fa works. REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://tbc-self-copy.preview.emergentagent.com.
seed_data_creation: None. Each test cleans up via PUT {url:'', enabled:true} and PATCH login_lockdown_enabled:false. Final env state confirmed clean (configured=false, lockdown=false).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Webhook bridge shipped + tested end-to-end. To re-run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://tbc-self-copy.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest backend/tests/test_webhook_ext.py -v. Tests use httpbin.org/post which is public — if that ever rate-limits in CI, swap to a local httptest server fixture. The send_event call sites (runtime_errors_ext, ai_test_bench_ext, deploy_projects_ext) are NOT exercised end-to-end by this iteration — they're only verified via the explicit /test endpoint and via static-code inspection of the imports. To test those code paths you'd need to trigger a real critical error / nightly drift / promote.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Feature ships clean. The original PUT/test pair, mask-host, https-only validation, kill-switch toggle, and clear flow all work first try; the FE card wires up cleanly to the BE; and the lockdown 503 paths still fire (the inline `from webhook_ext import send_event` + try/except wrapper is correctly defensive).
